title: AI Workflow Quick Teardown
audience: founders, operators, and restaurant teams who want a fast read before a larger diagnostic
pain: one workflow is leaking revenue, but a $499 diagnostic feels too large for the first step
deliverables: three concrete fixes|one recommended next action|upgrade credit toward the $499 diagnostic
proof_angle: Low-friction first purchase reduces cold checkout resistance

title: Restaurant AI Call Leak Diagnostic
audience: independent restaurant owners and restaurant-tech consultants
pain: missed calls lost catering inquiries and slow order handoff
deliverables: missed-call capture map|phone-agent/vendor shortlist|staff handoff workflow|first automation spec
proof_angle: clear ROI story around calls orders and staff interruptions

title: n8n Revenue Workflow Diagnostic
audience: founders, operators, and small agencies using n8n or Zapier
pain: leads enter forms, calendars, inboxes, and CRMs but do not become tracked follow-up
deliverables: lead routing audit|n8n workflow map|tracking and fallback checklist|implementation-ready backlog
proof_angle: Specific workflow pain beats a generic AI tools audit

title: OpenClaw Agent Ops Diagnostic
pain: agent demos fail to become reliable operating workflows
deliverables: agent boundary map|tool/action risk list|handoff architecture|first production workflow spec
proof_angle: Agent reliability is more urgent than another comparison page

title: QSR OpenClaw Agent Pack
audience: restaurant operators, food trucks, caterers, and restaurant-tech consultants
pain: missed calls, messy event inquiries, inventory reorder gaps, and manager follow-up are spread across phone, text, email, and spreadsheets
deliverables: missed-call capture template|ordering handoff template|inventory reorder watchlist|loyalty winback workflow|daily manager ops digest
proof_angle: George's same-day reply showed QSR buyers ask for proof and a concrete operator pack before larger setup
